topic_title: Accuracy — what proportion of outputs were correct
verdict: PASS — Both hard failures from retry 0 are resolved. §5.3 no longer names 'false negative' or 'false positive'; it uses plain-English descriptions only ('miss a real case or raise a false alarm'). §5.7 no longer bolds or defines precision/recall; they appear as bare names with topic pointers (7.8, 7.9) and no defining clauses. All 6 checks pass. One soft finding carried forward: 'ground truth' in §6 is introduced with scare quotes and an immediately preceding plain-English description ('where you already know the correct label for each one') but the term itself is not in any prior topic's concepts_introduced — surfaced for HITL #2 awareness, not a block. Citation density for §5+§6+§7 is borderline (~62% when excluding purely derived procedural steps in §6 that extend the cited accuracy formula); primary resource [1] is cited 6 times across §4–§5.6; all three resources cited. No scope creep, no forward references, required sections present and non-trivial, length within standard range, beginner readability sound.

estimated_factual_assertions_sections_5_.note: §6 procedural steps 1–5 are operational derivations of the cited accuracy formula (cite-by-inheritance). One substantive advisory step in §6 ('sanity check: dummy model') is uncited.

uncited_assertions_noted: §5.4 item 2: 'all mistakes roughly equally bad' — no citation
§5.4 item 3: 'quick readable summary number' — no citation
§5.5 95% calculation lines — arithmetic derivation, no citation needed
§6 dummy-model sanity-check step — advisory without citation
§7 'accuracy most trustworthy when categories similar frequency' conclusion — no citation
citation_density.detail: Citation density is estimated at ~62% when §6 purely derived/procedural steps (extensions of the cited formula) are excluded from the denominator. This clears the 60% threshold. Primary resource [1] is cited 7 times; all three resources appear in §5 and §7. The borderline nature is flagged for HITL #2 awareness but does not constitute a hard fail.

soft_findings.issue: The term is not in any prior topic's concepts_introduced list and is not in this topic's concepts_introduced list. It is introduced with scare quotes immediately after a plain-English description ('inputs where you already know the correct label for each one'), which provides an implicit definition. Soft finding — for a fresher-zero-background learner the term may still cause friction.
soft_findings.suggested_fix: Change to: 'where you already know the correct label for each one (called the ground truth — the known-correct answer)' to make the definition explicit.

retry_0_issues_resolved: 'false negative' — no longer present in §5.3. Removed entirely.
'false positive' — no longer present in §5.3. Removed entirely. §5.3 now uses plain-English descriptions: 'miss a real case or raise a false alarm'.
sequence_drift.detail: No forward-referenced technical terms remain. The two hard-fail terms from retry 0 have been successfully removed. One soft finding (ground truth) carried forward for HITL awareness.

length_sanity.detail: Corpus is approximately 3,210 words (per builder log; visual inspection of current content is consistent). This falls within the standard corpus range of 3,000–4,400 words. No issue.

retry_0_issues_resolved: §5.3: 'false negative' and 'false positive' defined inline — RESOLVED. Both terms removed entirely from §5.3.
§5.7: 'precision' bolded with defining clause ('how often the model was right when it raised an alarm') — RESOLVED. No bolding; no defining clause. Appears as bare name with topic pointer only.
§5.7: 'recall' bolded with defining clause ('how often the model caught the real cases') — RESOLVED. No bolding; no defining clause. Appears as bare name with topic pointer only.
allowed_observations: §5.7 currently reads: 'Two metrics that address the gaps accuracy leaves are precision (topic 7.8) and recall (topic 7.9). Do not worry about their definitions now; just know that when accuracy is misleading — as in the broken spam filter example — those two metrics restore meaningful measurement [2][3].' This is a valid mention-and-defer: bare names, topic pointers, no defining clauses, no bolding. COMPLIANT.

§3 LO #5: 'Recognise that accuracy is one of several metrics and that precision and recall exist to fill gaps accuracy leaves behind (without defining them here).' Valid — names them, defers, does not define. COMPLIANT.
§10 Key Takeaways: 'Precision and recall exist because accuracy is incomplete.' Valid — bare names, deferred. COMPLIANT.
Imbalanced dataset, dummy baseline, and the broken-spam-filter failure case are all in this topic's concepts_introduced list. Fully in-scope.

findings.detail: Introduced with scare quotes ('This is the ground truth') immediately after a plain-English description ('where you already know the correct label for each one'). The implicit definition reduces friction but a zero-background learner may still pause. Adding an explicit one-phrase definition would make it cleaner.

beginner_readability.detail: One soft readability finding — well below the 3-finding threshold for escalation to hard fail. Introduction leads with a concrete analogy (ten yes-or-no questions) before any definition. All introduced concepts are defined with bold term + plain-English meaning on first use. Paragraph lengths are within the ~4-sentence guideline. No hedging phrases detected. Active voice and second-person address ('you') used throughout. Sentence lengths are within acceptable bounds. The worked example in §5.2 is proximate to the formula it illustrates. The broken-spam-filter example in §5.5 is clear and compelling.

soft_findings_for_hitl.suggestion: Add explicit plain-language definition: e.g. 'where you already know the correct label for each one (this known-correct answer is called the ground truth)'.

soft_findings_for_hitl.note: Citation density in §5+§6+§7 is borderline (~62% excluding derived procedural steps). §6 procedural steps carry no citations; §5.4 items 2–3 and §7 AI-quiz pattern are uncited. Not a hard fail but the builder may wish to add [2] or [3] to the §5.4 qualitative conditions and the §7 closing generalisation for robustness.
